data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Sinks on Elevated Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is down by 3.5 bps on Wednesday to 4.136% amid rising safe-haven demand spurred by elevated geopolitical tensions. The yield on Treasuries moves in the opposite direction of their prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Growing unrest in Iran has captured the attention of President Trump, who said earlier this week that "help is on its way" for protesters. That could mark the beginning of American involvement in another Middle Eastern conflict, although Trump hasn't specified how he will address the situation yet. Furthermore, Trump has continued to express interest in acquiring Greenland, drawing pushback from several European leaders.

Stock Market Volatility Leads to Demand for Treasuries

Furthermore, both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) have pulled back in recent days, depressing investors' appetite for risk and shifting capital into safer assets, like bonds and commodities.

An inflation report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) this morning contributed to the market decline, showing Producer Price Index (PPI) rising by 3% annually, above the consensus estimate of 2.7%. While elevated inflation typically pushes yields higher because investors demand greater compensation for lost purchasing power, safe-haven demand dominated on Wednesday, sending yields lower.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stellantis (STLA) Targets 2026 as the Turning Point for Its U.S. Comeback
data.description:
Stellantis (STLA) CEO Antonio Filosa says that 2026 will be a critical year for turning the automaker around in the U.S., following several years of declining sales and market share for brands like Jeep, Ram, and Dodge. Since taking over as CEO in May, Filosa has shifted the company's focus back to its strongest U.S. brands and away from some of the all-electric plans pushed by his predecessor. Speaking at the Detroit Auto Show, he said that the company has a solid strategy in place, but real success will depend on how well it is executed. As a result, 2026 will be about proving the plan through action.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Filosa described this year as only the first step in reshaping Stellantis, which was created five years ago from the merger of Fiat Chrysler and France's PSA Group. While he did not share detailed plans yet, he said that the company will outline its full strategy at a capital markets day in the first half of the year. He also left the door open to possible changes in the company's large portfolio of brands, including weaker U.S. performers like Fiat and Alfa Romeo. However, he stressed that Stellantis intends to remain a single company rather than break itself up.

Looking ahead, Filosa said the next phase of the turnaround will be discussed at a meeting next week with more than 200 company executives, where they will focus on culture, strategy, and how to execute in 2026. Notably, these changes come as Stellantis' global sales have dropped sharply from 6.5 million vehicles in 2021 to 5.7 million in 2024. Additionally, in the U.S. alone, sales plunged by about 27% over that period, thereby pushing the company from fourth to sixth place in terms of market share.

Is STLA Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on STLA stock based on seven Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average STLA price target of $11.30 per share implies 11.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Recent Pullback from All-Time Highs Spurs OKLO Stock Bulls
data.description:
Nuclear power proponent Oklo, Inc. (OKLO) has lost nearly half of its market value since reaching an all-time high last October. Adding to the excitement, the stock jumped 17% last Friday after Oklo signed an agreement with Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) to develop a 1.2 GW power plant in Ohio to support Meta's regional data center operations.

data.title: Several Executives Sell Costco Stock (COST) to Start the New Year
data.description:
Several executives at Costco Wholesale (COST) have sold company stock to kick off the new year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Two senior executives at the warehouse club sold a combined $3.7 million worth of COST stock over the past few weeks, while another insider has filed to sell company shares that he owns. Russ Miller, a senior executive vice president at Costco, sold 1,500 shares on Jan. 9 at $916.32 each, according to a regulatory filing. Miller recently announced that he's retiring from Costco in February of this year.

Another executive vice president at Costco, Javier Polit, has sold 2,049 Costco shares at an average price of $867.21, followed by a smaller sale of 558 shares at $862.90 apiece. After the latest sales, Polit still owns 10,190 Costco shares worth nearly $10 million. A third executive vice president, James Klauer, has filed to sell 1,500 shares worth $1.4 million in coming days.

COST Stock Rises

The sales come with COST stock on an upswing to begin 2026. Through two weeks of the year, Costco's stock has gained 11%. The rise comes after the shares fell 5% last year versus a 17% increase for the benchmark S&P 500 index.

Costco's stock drifted lower in 2025 as the company grappled with U.S. tariffs and worries about its lofty valuation. However, COST stock has reversed higher since the retailer announced strong December sales, with both U.S. and global same-store sales rising significantly. Costco is next scheduled to report its quarterly financial results on March 5.

Is COST Stock a Buy?

Costco Wholesale's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 24 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 19 Buy, four Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average COST price target of $1,060.76 implies 11.54% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Massive Outage Impacts 180,000 Verizon (VZ) Customers in U.S.
data.description:
Verizon (VZ), the largest wireless internet provider in the U.S. said it is experiencing a massive outage that is impacting 180,000 of its customers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The telecommunications giant said that the issue involves wireless internet and data services throughout America. In a social media post, Verizon said: "Our engineers are engaged and are working to identify and solve the issue quickly."

The website DownDetector said that 179,000 Verizon customers had reported problems beginning at noon on Jan. 14. Many of the outages are occurring in major U.S. cities such as New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, and Seattle.

Verizon's Problem a Mystery

A smaller number of T-Mobile
TMUS

+1.24%

(about 1,800) and AT&T
T

+1.33%

(about 1,700) users also reported problems starting at noon, the site said. T-Mobile said it has confirmed that its network is operating normally, but that "due to Verizon's reported outage, our customers may not be able to reach someone with Verizon service at this time."

AT&T said its network was operating normally.

Anna Gomez, a commissioner at the Federal Communications Commission, said in a social media post that she was paying close attention to reports of the wireless outage. "I'll be asking our Consumer/Public Safety Bureaus to keep a close eye on this situation and investigate the source of this service disruption," she said.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Sell Off on Surprising Inflation Print as Retail Sales Shine
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Wednesday as investors digested new inflation and retail sales data.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In November, the Producer Price Index (PPI) climbed 0.2% month-over-month, rising from 0.1% in October and in line with the analyst estimate of 0.2%. However, PPI rose 3% year-over-year, topping the estimate of 2.7%. The PPI tracks changes in prices received by producers for goods and services and is often viewed as a leading indicator of consumer inflation. Core PPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, was unchanged from the prior month and advanced 3% year-over-year. Analysts had forecast growth of 0.2% and 2.7%, respectively.

A strong consumer spending update wasn't enough to lift the market. In November, retail sales increased 0.6% month-over-month, above the estimate of 0.5% and marking the highest rate of growth since July. At the same time, the U.S. Department of Commerce revised October's retail sales growth to a decline of 0.1% from 0%.

November is generally a strong month for sales, as it marks the beginning of the holiday shopping season and includes Black Friday. From November 1 to December 31, online consumer spending surged 6.8% from the prior year to a record high of $257.8 billion, according to Adobe (ADBE).

Meanwhile, the Supreme Court held its second opinion day of the year, although investors expecting a decision on the landmark Trump tariff case were left empty-handed. The next earliest date that SCOTUS could announce a ruling for the case is Tuesday, although uncertainty remains as the court doesn't preannounce the rulings it will address.

Still, President Trump's tariffs haven't resulted in higher inflation, according to Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia President Anna Paulson. "We've seen progress on underlying inflation that is likely to continue and there is no evidence to date that tariff-induced price increases are leading to broader inflation," she said in prepared remarks for an economic event in Philadelphia on Wednesday. Paulson added that her base case for 2026 involves inflation cooling down to the Fed's target of 2%, a stabilizing labor market, and economic growth of 2%.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.53% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 1.07%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why This Top Analyst Thinks Amazon's Ad Revenue Can Double by 2030
data.description:
Top TD Cowen analyst John Blackledge believes Amazon's (AMZN) advertising business is entering a powerful new phase. After reviewing his firm's latest U.S. ad buyer survey, he raised his price target on Amazon to $315 from $300 and reiterated a Buy rating. The new price target implies a 30% upside from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What the Ad Buyer Survey Showed

According to the survey results, more than 60% of advertisers already using Amazon said they expect to increase spending in 2026, which Blackledge sees as a clear sign that Amazon will gain ad share over time.

The five-star analyst also noted that advertisers are increasingly using Amazon's data to reach shoppers both on Amazon and across the wider internet. He expects this part of the business to continue taking share from competing ad platforms.

Another factor the analyst highlighted is Prime Video advertising. By adding ads to Prime Video, Amazon is giving brands access to large audiences through video, while keeping them inside Amazon's ecosystem.

Blackledge also sees artificial intelligence playing an important role in helping advertisers build and run more effective ads. He said GenAI tools are helping advertisers create and optimize ads more easily, leading to better results and supporting higher spending over time.

Putting all of this together, Blackledge estimates Amazon's advertising revenue can grow from about $68 billion in 2025 to nearly $142 billion by 2030, equal to a 16% yearly growth rate. He also expects Amazon's share of global digital advertising outside China to rise steadily over that period.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold?  

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 46 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $295.05 per share implies 21.62% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Target Stock (NYSE:TGT) Notches Up as it Runs Into ICE Troubles in Minneapolis
data.description:
It would be easy to think that only Home Depot (HD) is seeing issues connected to immigration enforcement of late. But that is not so, as retail giant Target (TGT) is facing troubles of its own. Customers are calling on the store chain to keep ICE operations out, putting it between a rock and a hard place. The news actually hit shareholders fairly well, who gave shares a fractional boost in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Officials from the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agency reportedly detained two Target employees in a store vestibule following reports of "verbal sparring" in the parking lot. One of the employees shouted, "I'm literally a U.S. citizen!" This did not stop said officials from hustling said employee into a van.

Both Target employees were found to be American citizens, and both were later released, though the Department of Homeland Security noted that one individual had been "…arrested for assaulting a federal law enforcement offers (sic)." The video in turn fired up outcry from parts of the general public, who followed up with protests outside stores. Target, in turn, responded in about the only way it could: new corporate training programs about what to do when interacting with immigration officials.

Exposed Source Code

This was not the end of Target's problems, but rather, only the beginning. Target also found itself exposed in the source code department, with several Gitea repositories emerging that contained Target internal code and developer documentation. It is unclear who was responsible for this breach, but Target employees reportedly confirmed that the code was genuine.

Customer data was safe, reports suggest, at least as far as this breach went. But the loss of source code safety represents a potentially much larger incident in the making. SecurityScorecard chief information security officer Steve Cobb noted, "Unlike breaches that focus on customer data, a compromise of development infrastructure exposes the blueprints of how a company's systems operate. Once engineering assets surface publicly, even briefly, the spread becomes extremely difficult to contain and can create opportunities for deeper compromise."

Is Target Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TGT stock based on eight Buys, 13 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 9.87% loss in its share price over the past year, the average TGT price target of $98.83 per share implies 9.88% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: OpenAI Signs $10B Deal with Chip Startup Cerebras for More Computing Power
data.description:
AI firm OpenAI has signed a large new deal with chip startup Cerebras Systems to secure more computing power for ChatGPT and its other AI tools. Under this agreement, OpenAI will use Cerebras-designed chips and buy up to 750 megawatts of computing capacity over the next three years, which The Wall Street Journal says is worth more than $10 billion. This comes as OpenAI now serves more than 900 million users every week and is running into serious limits on the amount of computing power it can access. It is also worth noting that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a personal investor in Cerebras, and the two companies first discussed working together back in 2017.

data.title: Worker Suspended After Trump Heckling, Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips
data.description:
Remember how legacy automaker Ford (F) was playing host to President Trump recently? Well, Trump took a tour around the plant, past some of the workers. Normally, that is a good time to keep your head down and your eyes on your work. But someone decided to weigh in, publicly, and get suspended for it. The resulting fracas sent Ford shares down fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The worker in question saw what he apparently believed was an opportunity to take Trump to task over the Jeffrey Epstein case, declaring that Trump was a "pedophile protector," among other, less audible things. President Trump responded to this overture with a familiar gesture known commonly as "the bird."

The White House, via representative Steven Cheung, noted, "A lunatic was wildly screaming expletives in a complete fit of rage, and the President gave an appropriate and unambiguous response." The United Auto Workers, meanwhile, noted that one of its members had indeed been suspended, and said nothing more beyond that. Ford itself declared that the incident was "…a very small part of the day," and most Ford employees were "…great."

Truck of the Year, Again

Meanwhile, the Ford Maverick Lobo managed to land a familiar honor once more. For the sixth time in a row, the Ford Maverick Lobo was declared the North American Truck of the Year. This has, reports note, never actually happened before, making the Ford Maverick Lobo a standout in its field for a completely different reason.

Ford vehicle line program director Pam Wylie noted, "The Maverick Lobo isn't just a new offering; it's a response to a movement. We saw our customers personalizing their trucks for on-road performance and style, and we decided to give them a factory-tuned option that carries the Built Ford Tough seal of approval. This award is a tribute to our customers who continue to inspire us every day."

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on three Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 40.5% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.77 per share implies 0.46% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson
